description: The same multi-statement intent as `multi-stmt-handler-colon-shorthand-pos`, lifted to a named function so the `:`-shorthand body is one call expression (`<Idle : startGame()>`) — does NOT fire E-MULTI-STATEMENT-HANDLER. W-DEAD-FUNCTION / W-PROGRAM-001 co-fire incidentally; not asserted.
rationale: §4.14 admits a single-expression `:`-shorthand body; the §34 row's fix for the multi-statement shape is to "switch to bare-body form or lift to a named function". `<Idle : startGame()>` is one call expression with no top-level `;`, so the code SHALL stay silent. This NEG also pins the validator's expression-internal-context rule at the `:`-shorthand locus: the two `;` inside the lifted `startGame()` body must NOT count as top-level hits in the state-child scan.
